title: Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete SOC 2 clause. SOC 2 CC2.1 Communication and Information — the composite posture score plus driver attribution is the internal communication of compliance status. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC2.1 (Internal communication) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.10: Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.10' running on index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete ISO 27001 clause. §9.1 Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation — weighted posture composites are a measurement artefact. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.9.10: Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.9.10' running on index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
| eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin d span=7d as week
| stats avg(score_0_100) as avg_score, latest(weight) as weight by week, domain
| eval contrib=avg_score*weight
| stats sum(contrib) as composite by week
| sort week
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(composite) as prev_composite
| eval wow_delta=round(composite-prev_composite,2)
| join type=left week [
    search index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
    | eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | bin d span=7d as week
    | stats avg(score_0_100) as s by week, domain
    | sort 0 week -s
    | dedup week
    | rename domain as top_driver_domain, s as top_driver_score
    | fields week, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
  ]
| table week, composite, wow_delta, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
description: A single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. Weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composite that trends over time and complements—not replaces—framework-specific panels.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2, ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes posture:domain_score. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize `weight` to sum to 1 per week in ETL if not already; (2) simplify driver logic in production with explicit `driver_domain` from upstream model instead of `dedup` shortcut; (3) governance committee approves weights annually; (4) document formula in methodology PDF; (5) never use this score as sole regulatory metric. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=posture:domain_score`.
visualization: Line chart (composite), Overlay (wow_delta), Single value (latest composite), Table (driver).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for a single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composit. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="posture:domain_score"` (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `weight` to sum to 1 per week in ETL if not already; (2) simplify driver logic in production with explicit `driver_domain` from upstream model instead of `dedup` shortcut; (3) governance committee approves weights annually; (4) document formula in methodology PDF; (5) never use this score as sole regulatory metric.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
| eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin d span=7d as week
| stats avg(score_0_100) as avg_score, latest(weight) as weight by week, domain
| eval contrib=avg_score*weight
| stats sum(contrib) as composite by week
| sort week
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(composite) as prev_composite
| eval wow_delta=round(composite-prev_composite,2)
| join type=left week [
    search index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
    | eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | bin d span=7d as week
    | stats avg(score_0_100) as s by week, domain
    | sort 0 week -s
    | dedup week
    | rename domain as top_driver_domain, s as top_driver_score
    | fields week, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
  ]
| table week, composite, wow_delta, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution** — A single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. Weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composite that trends over time and complements—not replaces—framework-specific panels.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="posture:domain_score"` (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: posture:domain_score. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="posture:domain_score", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **d** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **week, domain**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **contrib** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **week**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **wow_delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: avg_score, composite, contrib, d, domain, s, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (composite), Overlay (wow_delta), Single value (latest composite), Table (driver).
